SYSTEM_NAME.label: System Name
SYSTEM_NAME.description: Label displayed at the top of every page
SYSTEM_NAME.note: Label displayed at the top of every page

ENABLED_FEATURES.label: Enabled Features
ENABLED_FEATURES.description: Which Jethro features are visible to users?
ENABLED_FEATURES.note: Which Jethro features are visible to users?

DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS.label: Default Permissions
DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS.description: Permissions to grant to new user accounts by default
DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS.note: Permissions to grant to new user accounts by default

RESTRICTED_USERS_CAN_ADD.label: Restricted Users Can Add
RESTRICTED_USERS_CAN_ADD.description: Allow users with group/congregation restrictions to create new persons and families?
RESTRICTED_USERS_CAN_ADD.note: Allow users with group/congregation restrictions to create new persons and families?

SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINS.label: Session Timeout Mins
SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINS.description: Inactive sessions will be logged out after this number of minutes
SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINS.note: Inactive sessions will be logged out after this number of minutes

SESSION_MAXLENGTH_MINS.label: Session Maxlength Mins
SESSION_MAXLENGTH_MINS.description: Every session will be logged out this many minutes after login
SESSION_MAXLENGTH_MINS.note: Every session will be logged out this many minutes after login

2FA_REQUIRED_PERMS.label: 2fa Required Perms
2FA_REQUIRED_PERMS.description: Users who hold permission levels selected here will be required to complete 2-factor authentication at login.
2FA_REQUIRED_PERMS.note: Users who hold permission levels selected here will be required to complete 2-factor authentication at login.

2FA_EVEN_FOR_RESTRICTED_ACCTS.label: 2fa Even For Restricted Accts
2FA_EVEN_FOR_RESTRICTED_ACCTS.description: Require 2-factor auth even for accounts with group/congregation restrictions?
2FA_EVEN_FOR_RESTRICTED_ACCTS.note: Require 2-factor auth even for accounts with group/congregation restrictions?

2FA_TRUST_DAYS.label: 2fa Trust Days
2FA_TRUST_DAYS.description: Users can tick a box to skip 2-factor auth for this many days. Set to 0 to disable.
2FA_TRUST_DAYS.note: Users can tick a box to skip 2-factor auth for this many days. Set to 0 to disable.

REQUIRE_INITIAL_NOTE.label: Require Initial Note
REQUIRE_INITIAL_NOTE.description: Whether an initial note is required when adding new family
REQUIRE_INITIAL_NOTE.note: Whether an initial note is required when adding new family

DEFAULT_NOTE_STATUS.label: Default Note Status
DEFAULT_NOTE_STATUS.description: Default status when creating a new note
DEFAULT_NOTE_STATUS.note: Default status when creating a new note

NOTES_ORDER.label: Notes Order
NOTES_ORDER.description: Order to display person and family notes
NOTES_ORDER.note: Order to display person and family notes

LOCK_LENGTH.label: Lock Length
LOCK_LENGTH.description: Number of minutes users have to edit an object before their lock expires
LOCK_LENGTH.note: Number of minutes users have to edit an object before their lock expires

PERSON_LIST_SHOW_GROUPS.label: Person List Show Groups
PERSON_LIST_SHOW_GROUPS.description: Show all groups when listing persons?
PERSON_LIST_SHOW_GROUPS.note: Show all groups when listing persons?

NOTES_LINK_TO_EDIT.label: Notes Link To Edit
NOTES_LINK_TO_EDIT.description: Should the homepage notes list link to the edit-note page?
NOTES_LINK_TO_EDIT.note: Should the homepage notes list link to the edit-note page?

CHUNK_SIZE.label: Chunk Size
CHUNK_SIZE.description: Batch size to aim for when dividing lists of items
CHUNK_SIZE.note: Batch size to aim for when dividing lists of items

REPEAT_DATE_THRESHOLD.label: Repeat Date Threshold
REPEAT_DATE_THRESHOLD.description: When a roster has this many columns, show the date on the right as well as the left
REPEAT_DATE_THRESHOLD.note: When a roster has this many columns, show the date on the right as well as the left

ROSTER_WEEKS_DEFAULT.label: Roster Weeks Default
ROSTER_WEEKS_DEFAULT.description: Number of weeks to show in rosters by default
ROSTER_WEEKS_DEFAULT.note: Number of weeks to show in rosters by default

ADDRESS_STATE_OPTIONS.label: Address State Options
ADDRESS_STATE_OPTIONS.description: (Leave blank to hide the state field)
ADDRESS_STATE_OPTIONS.note: (Leave blank to hide the state field)

ADDRESS_STATE_LABEL.label: Address State Label
ADDRESS_STATE_LABEL.description: Label for the 'state' field. (Leave blank to hide the state field)
ADDRESS_STATE_LABEL.note: Label for the 'state' field. (Leave blank to hide the state field)

ADDRESS_POSTCODE_REGEX.label: Address Postcode Regex
ADDRESS_POSTCODE_REGEX.description: Regex to validate postcodes; eg /^[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]$/ for 4 digits
ADDRESS_POSTCODE_REGEX.note: Regex to validate postcodes; eg /^[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]$/ for 4 digits

HOME_TEL_FORMATS.label: Home Tel Formats
HOME_TEL_FORMATS.description: Valid formats for home phone; use X for a digit
HOME_TEL_FORMATS.note: Valid formats for home phone; use X for a digit

WORK_TEL_FORMATS.label: Work Tel Formats
WORK_TEL_FORMATS.description: Valid formats for work phone; use X for a digit
WORK_TEL_FORMATS.note: Valid formats for work phone; use X for a digit

MOBILE_TEL_FORMATS.label: Mobile Tel Formats
MOBILE_TEL_FORMATS.description: Valid formats for mobile phone; use X for a digit
MOBILE_TEL_FORMATS.note: Valid formats for mobile phone; use X for a digit

MEMBER_LOGIN_ENABLED.label: Member Login Enabled
MEMBER_LOGIN_ENABLED.description: Should church members be able to log in at <system_url>members ?
MEMBER_LOGIN_ENABLED.note: Should church members be able to log in at <system_url>members ?

MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_NAME.label: Member Rego Email From Name
MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_NAME.description: Sender name for member rego emails
MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_NAME.note: Sender name for member rego emails

MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.label: Member Rego Email From Address
MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.description: Sender address for member rego emails
MEMBER_REGO_EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.note: Sender address for member rego emails

MEMBERS_SEE_AGE_BRACKET.label: Members See Age Bracket
MEMBERS_SEE_AGE_BRACKET.description: Should members be able to see and edit the age bracket field?
MEMBERS_SEE_AGE_BRACKET.note: Should members be able to see and edit the age bracket field?

MEMBER_VISIBLE_FOLDERS.label: Member Visible Folders
MEMBER_VISIBLE_FOLDERS.description: Folders in Documents which, if they exist, are visible to Members. Separate multiple directories with a pipe (|) character.
MEMBER_VISIBLE_FOLDERS.note: Folders in Documents which, if they exist, are visible to Members. Separate multiple directories with a pipe (|) character.

MEMBER_REGO_HELP_EMAIL.label: Member Rego Help Email
MEMBER_REGO_HELP_EMAIL.description: Address that users can contact for assistance with member rego (optional)
MEMBER_REGO_HELP_EMAIL.note: Address that users can contact for assistance with member rego (optional)

MEMBER_REGO_FAILURE_EMAIL.label: Member Rego Failure Email
MEMBER_REGO_FAILURE_EMAIL.description: Address to notifiy when member rego fails
MEMBER_REGO_FAILURE_EMAIL.note: Address to notifiy when member rego fails

MEMBERS_SHARE_ADDRESS.label: Members Share Address
MEMBERS_SHARE_ADDRESS.description: Should addresses be visible in the members area?
MEMBERS_SHARE_ADDRESS.note: Should addresses be visible in the members area?

ROSTER_FEEDS_ENABLED.label: Roster Feeds Enabled
ROSTER_FEEDS_ENABLED.description: Whether users can access their roster assignments via an ical feed with secret URL
ROSTER_FEEDS_ENABLED.note: Whether users can access their roster assignments via an ical feed with secret URL

PUBLIC_AREA_ENABLED.label: Public Area Enabled
PUBLIC_AREA_ENABLED.description: Whether to allow public access to certain info at <system_url>public
PUBLIC_AREA_ENABLED.note: Whether to allow public access to certain info at <system_url>public

SHOW_SERVICE_NOTES_PUBLICLY.label: Show Service Notes Publicly
SHOW_SERVICE_NOTES_PUBLICLY.description: Should service notes be visible in the public area at <system_url>public?
SHOW_SERVICE_NOTES_PUBLICLY.note: Should service notes be visible in the public area at <system_url>public?

PUBLIC_ROSTER_SECRET.label: Public Roster Secret
PUBLIC_ROSTER_SECRET.description: Advanced: Only allow access to public rosters if the URL contains \"&secret=<this-secret>\"
PUBLIC_ROSTER_SECRET.note: Advanced: Only allow access to public rosters if the URL contains \"&secret=<this-secret>\"

BIBLE_URL.label: Bible Url
BIBLE_URL.description: URL Template for bible passage links, with the keyword __REFERENCE__
BIBLE_URL.note: URL Template for bible passage links, with the keyword __REFERENCE__

CCLI_SEARCH_URL.label: Ccli Search Url
CCLI_SEARCH_URL.description: URL Template for searching CCLI, with the keyword __TITLE__
CCLI_SEARCH_URL.note: URL Template for searching CCLI, with the keyword __TITLE__

CCLI_DETAIL_URL.label: Ccli Detail Url
CCLI_DETAIL_URL.description: URL Template for CCLI song details by song number, with the keyword __NUMBER__
CCLI_DETAIL_URL.note: URL Template for CCLI song details by song number, with the keyword __NUMBER__

CCLI_REPORT_URL.label: Ccli Report Url
CCLI_REPORT_URL.description: URL Template for reporting usage to CCLI by song number, with keyword __NUMBER__
CCLI_REPORT_URL.note: URL Template for reporting usage to CCLI by song number, with keyword __NUMBER__

POSTCODE_LOOKUP_URL.label: Postcode Lookup Url
POSTCODE_LOOKUP_URL.description: URL template for looking up postcodes, with the keyword __SUBURB__
POSTCODE_LOOKUP_URL.note: URL template for looking up postcodes, with the keyword __SUBURB__

MAP_LOOKUP_URL.label: Map Lookup Url
MAP_LOOKUP_URL.description: URL template for map links, with the keywords __ADDRESS_STREET__, __ADDRESS_SUBURB__, __ADDRESS_POSTCODE__, __ADDRESS_STATE__
MAP_LOOKUP_URL.note: URL template for map links, with the keywords __ADDRESS_STREET__, __ADDRESS_SUBURB__, __ADDRESS_POSTCODE__, __ADDRESS_STATE__

QR_CODE_GENERATOR_URL.label: Qr Code Generator Url
QR_CODE_GENERATOR_URL.description: URL template for generating QR codes, containing the placeholder __URL__
QR_CODE_GENERATOR_URL.note: URL template for generating QR codes, containing the placeholder __URL__

EMAIL_CHUNK_SIZE.label: Email Chunk Size
EMAIL_CHUNK_SIZE.description: When displaying mailto links for emails, divide into batches of this size
EMAIL_CHUNK_SIZE.note: When displaying mailto links for emails, divide into batches of this size

MULTI_EMAIL_SEPARATOR.label: Multi Email Separator
MULTI_EMAIL_SEPARATOR.description: When displaying mailto links for emails, separate addresses using this character
MULTI_EMAIL_SEPARATOR.note: When displaying mailto links for emails, separate addresses using this character

TASK_NOTIFICATION_ENABLED.label: Task Notification Enabled
TASK_NOTIFICATION_ENABLED.description: (This feature also requires the task_reminder.php script to be called by cron every 5 minutes)
TASK_NOTIFICATION_ENABLED.note: (This feature also requires the task_reminder.php script to be called by cron every 5 minutes)

TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_NAME.label: Task Notification From Name
TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_NAME.description: Name from which task notifications should be sent
TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_NAME.note: Name from which task notifications should be sent

TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_ADDRESS.label: Task Notification From Address
TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_ADDRESS.description: Email address from which task notifications should be sent
TASK_NOTIFICATION_FROM_ADDRESS.note: Email address from which task notifications should be sent

MAILCHIMP_API_KEY.label: Mailchimp Api Key
MAILCHIMP_API_KEY.description: API Key for Mailchimp integration. NB the mailchimp sync script must also be called regularly by cron.
MAILCHIMP_API_KEY.note: API Key for Mailchimp integration. NB the mailchimp sync script must also be called regularly by cron.

SMTP_PORT.label: Smtp Port
SMTP_PORT.description: Port to connect to the SMTP server. Usually 25, 465 for SSL, or 587 for TLS.
SMTP_PORT.note: Port to connect to the SMTP server. Usually 25, 465 for SSL, or 587 for TLS.

SMS_MAX_LENGTH.label: Sms Max Length
SMS_MAX_LENGTH.description: Character limit for SMS messages. 160 chars is usually a one-part SMS.
SMS_MAX_LENGTH.explanation: SMSes are billed per 160 characters (or 70 if emojis / unicode are present), so setting SMS_MAX_LENGTH to 160 keeps costs down by enforcing brevity. To allow longer messages increase SMS_MAX_LENGTH to 306 (2x SMS segments), 459 (3x), 612 (4x), 765 (5x) etc.

SMS_UNICODE_PERMITTED.label: Sms Unicode Permitted
SMS_UNICODE_PERMITTED.description: Whether to permit emojis and other unicode in SMSes.
SMS_UNICODE_PERMITTED.explanation:
Be aware that even a single Unicode character doubles the cost for messages >70 chars (shifts encoding from GSM 03.38 to UCS-2).

1 = allow all Unicode; 0 = GSM-only (non-GSM chars block sending); 'when_free' = allow Unicode for single-segment messages (≤70 chars), block for longer. This only affects whether Jethro allows Unicode — your upstream provider has to as well. Cellcast supports Unicode natively; 5CentSMS requires contacting support.
SMS_UNICODE_PERMITTED.note: Whether to permit emojis and other unicode in SMSes

SMS_HTTP_URL.label: Sms Http Url
SMS_HTTP_URL.description: URL of the SMS gateway to send SMSes through. Used by TemplateSmsProvider / legacy gateway.
SMS_HTTP_URL.note: URL of the SMS messaging service

SMS_HTTP_HEADER_TEMPLATE.label: Sms Http Header Template
SMS_HTTP_HEADER_TEMPLATE.description: Template for the HTTP headers of the POST request Jethro sends to the SMS gateway. Some gateways use headers for authentication.
SMS_HTTP_HEADER_TEMPLATE.note: Template for the headers of a request to the SMS messaging service

SMS_HTTP_POST_TEMPLATE.label: Sms Http Post Template
SMS_HTTP_POST_TEMPLATE.description: Template for the body of the HTTP POST request Jethro sends to the SMS gateway.
SMS_HTTP_POST_TEMPLATE.note: Template for the body of a request to the SMS messaging service

SMS_RECIPIENT_ARRAY_PARAMETER.label: Sms Recipient Array Parameter
SMS_RECIPIENT_ARRAY_PARAMETER.description: Base name for the _RECIPIENTS_ARRAY_ keyword in SMS_HTTP_POST_TEMPLATE.

SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.label: Sms Http Response Ok Regex
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.description: Regex to detect a per-recipient success message from the SMS gateway. Omit slash delimiters.
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.explanation:
Keywords: _RECIPIENT_, _RECIPIENT_INTERNATIONAL_.
Leave blank to ignore the response.

Example for 5CentSMS: [{]\s*"destination":\s*"_RECIPIENT_",[^}]*"status":\s*"?(1000|1001|1002|1004|1006|1011|1527)"?,
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.note: Regex for recognising a successful send

SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ERROR_REGEX.label: Sms Http Response Error Regex
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ERROR_REGEX.description: Regex to detect an overall error response from the API server (e.g. bad credentials). Omit slash delimiters. Leave blank to ignore.
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ERROR_REGEX.note: Regex for recognising an API error

SMS_LOCAL_PREFIX.label: Sms Local Prefix
SMS_LOCAL_PREFIX.description: Local prefix used in the mobile numbers stored in Jethro. Stripped before prepending the international prefix.
SMS_LOCAL_PREFIX.explanation: Needed for SMS gateways that use international-format numbers. E.g. 0.
SMS_LOCAL_PREFIX.note: Used for converting local to international numbers.  eg 0

SMS_INTERNATIONAL_PREFIX.label: Sms International Prefix
SMS_INTERNATIONAL_PREFIX.description: International prefix, which replaces the local prefix to form an international-format number.
SMS_INTERNATIONAL_PREFIX.explanation: Needed for SMS gateways that use international-format numbers. E.g. 61 for Australia.
SMS_INTERNATIONAL_PREFIX.note: Used for converting local to international numbers. eg +61

SMS_SEGMENT_COST.label: Sms Segment Cost
SMS_SEGMENT_COST.description: Cost per SMS segment in dollars (e.g. 0.05). Overrides the provider's default rate for cost estimates in the compose UI and the Messages history. Use this if you have a bulk-purchase discount or non-standard pricing agreement.
SMS_SEGMENT_COST.note: Cost per SMS segment in dollars (e.g. 0.05). Overrides the provider default.

SMS_ENCODING.label: Sms Encoding
SMS_ENCODING.description: Typed text is stripped to be representable in this encoding before being sent. GSM0338 is the default and only valid value.
SMS_ENCODING.note: Sms text is forced into this encoding before sending.

SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.label: Sms Http Response Id Regex
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.description: Regex with a capture group to extract the remote message ID from the gateway response.
SMS_HTTP_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.explanation: The first capture group is stored as the remote message ID.

SMS_SEND_LOGFILE.label: Sms Send Logfile
SMS_SEND_LOGFILE.description: Path to a JSONP log file for SMS send audit trail (optional).

SMS_SEGMENT_LENGTH.label: Sms Segment Length
SMS_SEGMENT_LENGTH.description: Length of a single SMS segment for cost estimation purposes

DB_DATABASE.label: Db Database
DB_DATABASE.description: Name of the MySQL database, e.g. jethro.

DB_USERNAME.label: Db Username
DB_USERNAME.description: Username of the MySQL account Jethro uses to access the database.

DB_PASSWORD.label: Db Password
DB_PASSWORD.description: Password of the MySQL account Jethro uses to access the database.
DB_PASSWORD.explanation: Optional if DB_DSN is set for socket-based authentication.
DB_PASSWORD.note: MySQL password. Optional if DB_DSN is set.

DB_HOST.label: Db Host
DB_HOST.description: Server on which MySQL is running — usually localhost.

DB_DSN.label: Db Dsn
DB_DSN.description: Full PDO DSN instead of DB_PASSWORD, e.g. mysql:dbname=jethro.
DB_DSN.explanation: For socket-based authentication. If your PHP-FPM runs as a dedicated OS user (jethro), you can create a MySQL user with unix_socket auth and specify the DSN directly instead of a password.
DB_DSN.note: Full PDO DSN instead of DB_PASSWORD, e.g. mysql:dbname=jethro. For socket-based auth.

BASE_URL.label: Base Url
BASE_URL.description: The URL Jethro runs at. Only needed if behind a proxy without X-Forwarded-Proto / X-Forwarded-Host, or when running CLI scripts like task_reminder.php where the base URL cannot be inferred.
BASE_URL.note: URL Jethro runs at. Set if behind a proxy without X-Forwarded-Proto / X-Forwarded-Host.

DB_MEMBERS_USERNAME.label: Db Members Username
DB_MEMBERS_USERNAME.description: MySQL username for the Members area. Restrict table access for extra security.
DB_MEMBERS_USERNAME.explanation: Defaults to the main DB_USERNAME if not set.

DB_MEMBERS_PASSWORD.label: Db Members Password
DB_MEMBERS_PASSWORD.description: MySQL password for the Members area.
DB_MEMBERS_PASSWORD.explanation: Defaults to the main DB_PASSWORD if not set.

DB_PUBLIC_USERNAME.label: Db Public Username
DB_PUBLIC_USERNAME.description: MySQL username for the Public area. Restrict table access for extra security.
DB_PUBLIC_USERNAME.explanation: Defaults to the main DB_USERNAME if not set.

DB_PUBLIC_PASSWORD.label: Db Public Password
DB_PUBLIC_PASSWORD.description: MySQL password for the Public area.
DB_PUBLIC_PASSWORD.explanation: Defaults to the main DB_PASSWORD if not set.

2FA_ENABLED.label: 2fa Enabled
2FA_ENABLED.description: Master kill-switch for 2-factor authentication. Set to false to disable 2FA entirely.
2FA_ENABLED.explanation: Setting to true still requires other 2FA parameters to be defined for 2FA to be applied.
2FA_ENABLED.note: Master kill-switch. Set to false to disable 2FA entirely.

2FA_SMS_URL.label: 2fa Sms Url
2FA_SMS_URL.description: Dedicated SMS gateway URL for 2FA messages. If set, 2FA uses these 2FA_SMS_* settings instead of SMS_HTTP_*.
2FA_SMS_URL.explanation: 2FA messages sent through this gateway are NOT logged in SMS_SEND_LOGFILE.

2FA_SMS_POST_TEMPLATE.label: 2fa Sms Post Template
2FA_SMS_POST_TEMPLATE.description: POST body template for the dedicated 2FA SMS gateway.

2FA_SMS_HEADER_TEMPLATE.label: 2fa Sms Header Template
2FA_SMS_HEADER_TEMPLATE.description: Extra HTTP headers for the dedicated 2FA SMS gateway.

2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.label: 2fa Sms Response Ok Regex
2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_OK_REGEX.description: Success detection regex for the dedicated 2FA SMS gateway. Omit slash delimiters.

2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_ERROR_REGEX.label: 2fa Sms Response Error Regex
2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_ERROR_REGEX.description: Error detection regex for the dedicated 2FA SMS gateway. Omit slash delimiters.

2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.label: 2fa Sms Response Id Regex
2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.description: Remote message ID regex for the dedicated 2FA SMS gateway.
2FA_SMS_RESPONSE_ID_REGEX.note: Remote message ID regex for 2FA messages.

SMS_2FA_LOGFILE.label: Sms 2fa Logfile
SMS_2FA_LOGFILE.description: Path to a log file for the 2FA audit trail. Example: /var/log/jethro-2fa.log.
SMS_2FA_LOGFILE.note: Path to JSONP log file for 2FA audit trail.

SMS_2FA_LOG_LEVEL.label: Sms 2fa Log Level
SMS_2FA_LOG_LEVEL.description: Log verbosity for 2FA audit trail: full for everything, or quiet for errors only.

SMS_2FA_BLOCK_MESSAGES.label: Sms 2fa Block Messages
SMS_2FA_BLOCK_MESSAGES.description: Dev-only: log 2FA intentions without actually sending SMS messages.

OVERRIDE_EMAIL_FROM.label: Override Email From
OVERRIDE_EMAIL_FROM.description: Force the From: address on Jethro-sent emails. The user-specified From becomes Reply-To instead.
OVERRIDE_EMAIL_FROM.explanation: Use this when the From address (set e.g. in a script .ini file) is where you want replies to go, but you are not DKIM-authorised to send from its domain.
OVERRIDE_EMAIL_FROM.note: Force the From: address on Jethro-sent emails. The user-specified From becomes Reply-To instead.

MEMBER_LOGIN_NOTE.label: Member Login Note
MEMBER_LOGIN_NOTE.description: HTML displayed beneath the Members Area login form, e.g. a link to terms and conditions.
MEMBER_LOGIN_NOTE.note: HTML beneath the member login form.

MEMBER_FILES_DIRS.label: Member Files Dirs
MEMBER_FILES_DIRS.description: Pipe-separated document folder paths visible to members. Paths are relative to DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.
MEMBER_FILES_DIRS.explanation: Generally this should be left unset — use the 'Member Visible Folders' web config setting instead.
MEMBER_FILES_DIRS.note: Pipe-separated document folder paths visible to members.

BIBLE_API_APIKEY.label: Bible Api Apikey
BIBLE_API_APIKEY.description: API key from api.bible. Empty = Bible passage auto-fetch disabled.
BIBLE_API_APIKEY.explanation: Obtainable after registering on api.bible. Enables automatic fetching of Bible reading passages in service handouts.
BIBLE_API_APIKEY.note: API key from api.bible. Empty = disabled.

BIBLE_API_URL.label: Bible Api Url
BIBLE_API_URL.description: Base URL for the API.Bible REST API, used for fetching Bible passage text in service handouts.
BIBLE_API_URL.explanation: Defaults to the standard API.Bible endpoint if not set.

BIBLE_API_TIMEOUT.label: Bible Api Timeout
BIBLE_API_TIMEOUT.description: Timeout in seconds for API.Bible REST API requests.

ALLOW_SETTINGS_IN_FILE.label: Allow Settings In File
ALLOW_SETTINGS_IN_FILE.description: Allow all configuration settings to be defined in conf.php rather than via the admin web interface (legacy mode).
ALLOW_SETTINGS_IN_FILE.note: Allow all settings to be defined in conf.php rather than via the admin web interface (legacy mode).

DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.label: Documents Root Path
DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.description: Directory where the Documents view stores files.
DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.explanation: Defaults to files/ under the Jethro root directory.
DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.note: Document storage path. Defaults to files/ under Jethro root.

CUSTOM_REPORTS_DIR.label: Custom Reports Dir
CUSTOM_REPORTS_DIR.description: Directory where custom report SQL files are stored.
CUSTOM_REPORTS_DIR.explanation: Defaults to custom_reports/ under the Jethro root directory.
CUSTOM_REPORTS_DIR.note: Custom report SQL directory. Defaults to custom_reports/ under Jethro root.

SERVICE_DOCS_TO_POPULATE_DIRS.label: Service Docs To Populate Dirs
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_POPULATE_DIRS.description: Pipe-separated folder paths for the 'Populate' feature when generating service documents.
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_POPULATE_DIRS.explanation: Paths can be absolute or relative to DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_POPULATE_DIRS.note: Pipe-separated template folders for the Populate feature.

SERVICE_DOCS_TO_EXPAND_DIRS.label: Service Docs To Expand Dirs
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_EXPAND_DIRS.description: Pipe-separated folder paths for the 'Expand' feature when generating service documents.
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_EXPAND_DIRS.explanation: Paths can be absolute or relative to DOCUMENTS_ROOT_PATH.
SERVICE_DOCS_TO_EXPAND_DIRS.note: Pipe-separated template folders for the Expand feature.

SYSADMIN_HREF.label: Sysadmin Href
SYSADMIN_HREF.description: System administrator contact link — mailto:someone@domain.com or a URL.
SYSADMIN_HREF.note: System administrator contact link — mailto:someone@domain.com or a URL.

ERRORS_EMAIL_ADDRESS.label: Errors Email Address
ERRORS_EMAIL_ADDRESS.description: Email address where error reports are sent.

SQL_MODE.label: Sql Mode
SQL_MODE.explanation: If MySQL 8 throws ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY errors, turn it off with: STRICT_TRANS_TABLES,NO_ZERO_IN_DATE,NO_ZERO_DATE,ERROR_FOR_DIVISION_BY_ZERO,NO_ENGINE_SUBSTITUTION
SQL_MODE.note: Override MySQL SQL_MODE. Use if MySQL 8 throws ONLY_FULL_GROUP_BY errors.

PREFILL_USERNAME.label: Prefill Username
PREFILL_USERNAME.description: Pre-filled username on the login form. Demo systems only.
PREFILL_USERNAME.note: Pre-filled login form value. Demo systems only.

PREFILL_PASSWORD.label: Prefill Password
PREFILL_PASSWORD.description: Pre-filled password on the login form. Demo systems only.
PREFILL_PASSWORD.note: Pre-filled login form value. Demo systems only.

EXTRA_HEAD_HTML.label: Extra Head Html
EXTRA_HEAD_HTML.description: HTML injected into <head> on every page, e.g. custom CSS <link> tags.
EXTRA_HEAD_HTML.note: HTML injected into <head>, e.g. custom CSS <link> tags.

CUSTOM_LESS_VARS.label: Custom Less Vars
CUSTOM_LESS_VARS.description: Override LESS variables for a custom colour scheme.
CUSTOM_LESS_VARS.note: Override LESS variables for custom colour scheme, e.g. @linkColor: #008791;.

LOGIN_NOTE.label: Login Note
LOGIN_NOTE.description: HTML displayed beneath the Control Centre login button, e.g. a link to terms and conditions.
LOGIN_NOTE.note: HTML beneath the Control Centre login button.

SHOW_ERROR_DETAILS.label: Show Error Details
SHOW_ERROR_DETAILS.description: Show error details in the browser. Turn off in production.
SHOW_ERROR_DETAILS.note: Show error details in the browser.

2FA_SENDER_ID.label: 2fa Sender Id
2FA_SENDER_ID.description: Sender ID for 2-factor auth messages. Mirrors SMS_SENDER.
2FA_SENDER_ID.note: Sender ID for 2-factor auth messages. Mirrors SMS_SENDER.

icalFeeds.heading: iCal feeds
icalFeeds.intro: Whether users can access their roster assignments via an iCal feed with a secret URL.

messageBody.heading: Message body
messageBody.intro: Limits on what the user can type.

legacyGateway.heading: Legacy HTTP template gateway
legacyGateway.intro: Only needed if using TemplateSmsProvider, FiveCentSmsV4Provider, or a custom HTTP-template gateway. Most users should use one of the provider-specific sections above instead.

twoFactorSmsGateway.heading: 2FA SMS gateway (file-only)
twoFactorSmsGateway.intro: If you want 2FA to use a different SMS gateway than regular sends. When unset, 2FA falls back to the regular SMS constants.

essential.heading: Essential
essential.intro: These must be set for the system to run.

databaseAccounts.heading: Database accounts (optional)
databaseAccounts.intro: Separate MySQL credentials for the Members and Public areas, to restrict which tables those interfaces can access. All default to the main credentials.

bibleApi.heading: Bible API (file-only)
bibleApi.intro: Auto-fetches Bible passage text for service handouts. Requires a key from api.bible.

sms.heading: SMS
sms.intro: SMS messaging configuration — message limits, gateway templates, and 2FA transport.
